qs.subscribeDesc: Subscribe to tournaments for quick access to players, pairings and results.
qs.monitorDesc: Enable the monitor on a tournament — new rounds are detected and crawled automatically.
qs.favoritesDesc: Mark players or teams as favorites for quick access in the tournament view.
qs.chessResultsDesc: Add your ChessResults ID in your profile — upcoming tournaments are subscribed automatically.

login.title: Login
login.required: Please log in or register to continue.

discord.title: Discord
discord.hint: Open a personal link from the RookHub Discord bot (its /link command or a puzzle DM) to connect your Discord account.

discord.stashed: Sign in or register to finish linking your Discord account.

tokens.title: Extension Tokens
tokens.subtitle: Personal access tokens for machine clients (e.g. chess.com extension).
tokens.empty: No tokens yet. Create one if you want to connect the chess.com extension.
tokens.revokeConfirm: Revoke token "{{name}}"? The client will no longer be able to connect.

show.title: Token created
show.warning: This token is shown ONLY now, once. Copy it into your extension — it cannot be retrieved later.

offline.title: Offline
offline.hint: Store puzzles on this device to keep solving without internet (per device).

delete.title: Delete account
delete.warn: Warning: your identity and personal data will be irreversibly removed. Please enter your password to confirm.

accountDeletion.title: Delete account
accountDeletion.intro: This page explains how to delete your RookHub account and what happens to your data.
accountDeletion.inApp: Sign in to the app and go to Profile → "Delete account". Confirm with your password. The account is deleted immediately.
accountDeletion.removed1: Your identity and personal data (username, email, display/real name, FIDE/ChessResults ID, Chess.com/Lichess name, Discord link) and login access.
accountDeletion.removed2: Personal content and links: repertoires, tournament subscriptions/favourites, friendships and group memberships.
accountDeletion.kept: Aggregated solving/play statistics (e.g. solved puzzles) are retained in anonymised form that cannot be traced back to you.
accountDeletion.contact: For deletion requests or questions, reach us at

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: RookHub is a chess training platform (puzzles, endless mode, courses, repertoires). This policy explains how personal data is processed under the GDPR.
privacy.controller: The controller for data processing is the operator named in the imprint
privacy.dataIntro: Depending on your use, we process:
privacy.dataAccount: Account data: username, email address, password (stored only as a hash).
privacy.dataProfile: Profile data (optional): display/real name, FIDE ID, ChessResults ID, Chess.com/Lichess username, optional Discord link.
privacy.dataUsage: Usage data: solved puzzles, endless/course progress and statistics, play rating, login timestamps.
privacy.dataTechnical: Technical data: IP address, requested endpoints with timestamps and a pseudonymous visitor identifier (reach measurement) in server logs.
privacy.purposes: Providing the account and features (performance of a contract, Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) as well as operational security, error analysis and reach measurement via logs (legitimate interest, Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).
privacy.thirdIntro: Data is not sold. Depending on your use, the following services are involved:
privacy.thirdDiscord: Discord: only on voluntary account linking (Discord ID/name) for display in the RookHub Discord bot.
privacy.thirdChessresults: chess-results.com: tournament data is fetched server-side when you set a ChessResults ID or load tournaments.
privacy.thirdChesssites: Chess.com / Lichess: only the usernames you voluntarily enter; no automatic data transfer.
privacy.thirdLogging: Elasticsearch/Kibana: self-hosted logging/monitoring on our infrastructure.
privacy.thirdHosting: Hosting: operated on our own server infrastructure with a TLS reverse proxy.
privacy.storage: We do not use tracking cookies. Browser storage (localStorage) holds e.g. your login token, settings and — if enabled — offline puzzles. These remain on your device.
privacy.retention: Account data is stored until you delete your account. You can delete your account at any time — identity and personal data are removed, anonymised statistics are retained. More:
privacy.rights: You have the right to access, rectification, erasure, restriction, data portability and objection, as well as the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (in Austria: the Data Protection Authority).
privacy.contact: For privacy questions, reach us at

impressum.title: Imprint
impressum.disclaimer: Note: the service provider details are placeholders and must be replaced with the actual data before publication. This page is a draft and does not constitute legal advice.

help.title: Endless Puzzle Mode
help.gameplayP1: You solve chess puzzles of increasing difficulty. You start at a configurable rating and work your way up. The rating rises automatically along the Fasttrack phases (see below).
help.gameplayP2: You have 3 lives. At 0 lives the game is over.
help.movesP1: Each puzzle has an intended solution (a sequence of moves). As long as you play the right moves, you follow that solution line.
help.movesP2: If you play a wrong move, Stockfish (a chess engine) takes over the other side. You then play on freely against Stockfish. Its strength can be set via the Stockfish Depth.
help.movesItem1: Checkmating Stockfish counts as an alternative solution — no life lost!
help.movesItem2: Stockfish checkmates you — you lose a life
help.movesItem3: The game against Stockfish continues endlessly until checkmate or Give Up
help.buttonsShowEval: Show Eval — Shows the Stockfish evaluation (Start vs. Now)
help.buttonsReset: Reset — Resets the puzzle, but costs a life
help.buttonsMouseslip: Mouseslip — Undoes the last wrong move (once per puzzle, free)
help.settingsStartRating: Start Rating — The puzzle rating you start at
help.settingsStockfishDepth: Stockfish Depth — Engine strength (1 = very weak, 24 = maximum strength)
help.settingsThemes: Themes — Optional: only certain tactical themes (e.g. "fork pin")
help.fasttrackP1: The increase adapts to your past performance: it analyses your recent sessions and calculates the rating at which you typically lose lives.
help.fasttrackPhase1: Phase 1 (Puzzles 1–5) — Fast increase up to your 1st threshold
help.fasttrackPhase2: Phase 2 (Puzzles 6–10) — Increase from the 1st to the 2nd threshold
help.fasttrackP2: The threshold values (1st/2nd threshold) are calculated automatically but can be adjusted manually. Click "Auto: …" to reset the value.

config.title: Endless Puzzle Mode
config.subtitle: Progressive difficulty — how far can you go?
config.thresholdExplain: Ratings where you typically lose lives. Adjust to skip easier puzzles faster or slower.
config.curveHint: Rating curve of the gauntlet (fixed when you start):
config.unfinishedRun: Unfinished run: Lv {{level}} | {{solved}} solved | {{lives}} lives | {{max}} max

puzzles.title: Puzzles
puzzles.backfillHint: Builds the normalized tag table (Tags / PuzzleTags) used by the fast theme filter ("train worst themes"). Run once after deploying — the import keeps tags up to date afterwards. Runs as a background job.
puzzles.backfillConfirm: Build / refresh the puzzle tag table now? This runs as a background job.
puzzles.backfillStarted: Tag backfill started (running in the background).

install.title: Install RookHub on Android
install.intro: RookHub is available as an Android app. To install it:
install.step1: Tap “Download APK” below — ideally directly on your Android device.
install.step2: Open the downloaded file. If prompted, allow “Install from unknown sources” for your browser.
install.step3: Confirm the installation — done. RookHub appears as an app in your launcher.
install.iosNote: Android only. On iPhone/iPad, open the site in Safari and use “Share → Add to Home Screen” to install it as an app.
